items.title: Your LLM issues are really data issues
items.raw_content: Ryan welcomes Harsha Chintalapani, co-founder and CTO at Collate and co-creator of Open Metadata, to the show to discuss why AI and LLMs struggle with real-time, structured production data.
items.ai_summary: 播客探讨AI和LLM在处理实时结构化生产数据时遇到的困难，指出问题根源在于数据而非模型。

items.title: Turning scattered knowledge into trusted intelligence: Stack Internal 2026.3
items.raw_content: Ingestion is now generally available in Stack Internal as part of the 2026.3 release, allowing you to transform content siloed across your tools into structured, verified knowledge that's ready for your teams and AI.
items.ai_summary: Stack Internal 2026.3版本发布，其数据摄取功能正式可用，可将分散在各工具中的内容转化为结构化、可信的知识，供团队和AI使用。

items.title: Welcome to the “find out” stage of AI
items.raw_content: AI companies are looking a little different after going through renewal cycle.
items.ai_summary: AI公司在经历续约周期后呈现出不同面貌，标志着AI进入了“发现”阶段。

items.title: Lights, camera, open source!
items.raw_content: Ryan is joined on the show by Cult.Repo producers Emma Tracey and Josiah McGarvie to discuss making documentaries about open-source software and the people behind the major technologies that uphold the internet.
items.ai_summary: 播客邀请Cult.Repo制作者讨论开源软件纪录片，讲述开源技术和维护者故事。

items.title: Black box AI drift: AI tools are making design decisions nobody asked for
items.raw_content: Prompts go in, output comes out, and the decisions made in between are hidden from view.
items.ai_summary: AI工具的黑箱特性导致其做出了无人要求的决策，设计决策隐藏在提示和输出之间。

items.title: How to get multiple agents to play nice at scale
items.raw_content: Chase Roossin, group engineering manager, and Steven Kulesza, staff software engineer, from Intuit join the podcast to chat about what might be the hardest problem in engineering right now: getting multiple AI agents to work together in a complex system.
items.ai_summary: Intuit工程师在播客中探讨如何让多个AI代理在复杂系统中协同工作，这是当前工程领域最困难的问题之一。

items.title: We still need developer communities
items.raw_content: Ryan welcomes Mike Swift, co-founder and CEO of Major League Hacking, to the show to chat about the never-ending need for software developer communities and entry points into programming; MHL’s recent acquisition of DEV and how they’re creating a place for shared knowledge, building, and publishing; and why now is the best time to be both an artisan and a builder in a world with AI software development tools.
items.ai_summary: Major League Hacking CEO讨论开发者社区的持续需求，MLH收购DEV后计划创建知识共享、构建和发布的平台，并肯定AI工具时代手工艺人和构建者价值。

items.title: No country left behind with sovereign AI
items.raw_content: Ryan welcomes Stephen Watt, distinguished engineer and VP of Red Hat’s Office of the CTO, to chat about digital sovereignty and sovereign AI.
items.ai_summary: Red Hat首席技术官办公室杰出工程师讨论数字主权和主权AI，确保各国不被落下。

items.title: Why AI hasn't replaced human expertise—and what that means for your SaaS stack
items.raw_content: The most valuable AI tools in your enterprise stack do more than generate answers. They help developers determine which answers to trust.
items.ai_summary: 企业中最有价值的AI工具不仅仅是生成答案，还能帮助开发者确定哪些答案值得信任。

items.title: Human input needed: take our survey on AI agents
items.raw_content: Are you still "human-in-the-loop," or have you moved to "human-on-the-loop," overseeing a bot that’s doing the driving?
items.ai_summary: Stack Overflow发起关于AI代理的调查，询问人类是从循环中参与还是仅监督。
